title: Jonathan Litardo’s CV
text:
Cybersecurity Student | Data Science Certificate | CompTIA Security+

lit23005@byui.edu | LinkedIn | GitHub 



Pursuing a B.S. in Cybersecurity with a Data Science Certificate at Brigham Young University-Idaho.


Incident response, threat detection, network monitoring, vulnerability assessment, risk analysis, log correlation



Capture the Flag (CTF) competitions, cloud security architecture, open-source security tooling




March 2027 (expected) Brigham Young University - Idaho, Rexburg, ID

Bachelor of Science in Cybersecurity, Minor in IT Fundamentals, Data Science Certificate
Relevant Coursework: Network Security Analysis, Incident Response, Threat Intelligence, Security Operations, Security Platform

March 2021 Jose Domingo de Santistevan Technical High School, Guayaquil, Ecuador

Technical Diploma, focus in Object-Oriented Programming, Network Infrastructure, Virtualization




July 2025 CompTIA Security+ (SY0-701)



April 2025 - June 2025 CYBER301 Network Security Lab, Brigham Young University - Idaho

Reduced false positive rate by 40% in network threat classification by analyzing 1,000+ daily packets in Wireshark and building custom detection signatures
Conducted vulnerability assessments using Kali Linux penetration testing tools to identify critical security weaknesses across simulated enterprise networks
Contained and documented 15+ simulated breach scenarios by executing NIST-aligned incident response playbooks including containment, eradication, and lessons-learned reporting
Developed custom detection queries and generated detailed technical reports documenting threat patterns and remediation recommendations
Collaborated with team members to correlate security logs and identify attack vectors in multi-stage breach scenarios




April 2025 - Present Plumber Assistant, BYU-Idaho Mechanical Operations, Rexburg, ID

Resolved 5-10 weekly maintenance requests with a 95%+ on-time completion rate by triaging urgency and coordinating cross-building schedules
Performed 8+ critical installations including toilets and faucets in student housing, ensuring compliance with plumbing codes and safety standards
Eliminated preventable water damage events by implementing proactive inspection checklists for 10+ building systems, reducing emergency callouts

April 2024 - December 2025 Custodial Technician, BYU-Idaho Facilities Services, Rexburg, ID

Maintained service continuity across 5+ facilities during off-peak shifts by independently managing workload without direct supervision, meeting 100% of scheduled deadlines
Cut supply retrieval time by reorganizing inventory systems across multiple shifts, improving coordination between 10+ team members





Security Operations: Incident Response, Threat Detection, Network Monitoring, Vulnerability Assessment, Risk Analysis, Log Correlation
Security Platforms: Wireshark, Nmap, Kali Linux, SIEM Tools, Active Directory, Endpoint Security
Technical Proficiencies: Linux Command Line, Virtual Machines, Docker, GitHub Actions CI/CD, AWS EC2
Programming: Python, JavaScript, SQL, HTML/CSS, React, PowerShell (basic), Bash, C++
Security Frameworks: NIST Cybersecurity Framework, Defense in Depth, CIA Triad, OWASP Top 10





Languages: English (Fluent), Spanish (Native)
Active participant in Capture The Flag (CTF) competitions, studying cloud security architectures, and contributing to open-source security tool development
